body.title: Librarian write path: tag extraction, embedding, typed payloads, source_agent
body.result: write_memory(): 1 Haiku tag call + Scraps embedding → EmbeddingPayload in payloads JSONB + source_agent provenance. Correct clan.memories columns. 17 tests green.
body.description:
Implement the Librarian memory write endpoint that compiles understanding at write time so retrieval is cheap. Write path: (1) extract tags via one inference call; (2) call Scraps embedding engine to compute and store embedding as EmbeddingPayload in payloads jsonb; (3) accept typed payloads (code, link, primitive) from caller; (4) record source_agent for provenance. Supersedes T-memory-agent-write-api (close that ticket on sprint start).

Key principle: inference fires once at write time. Everything stored is pre-computed. Future recall(X) calls use stored embeddings and tags with no write-time inference.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/ (write endpoint, new or extended)
**Design rules:** inference at write time only — not query time; tag extraction is one inference call total, not per-tag; source_agent is required field; supersedes T-memory-agent-write-api
**Scope boundary:** write path only; retrieval (recall(X)) is T-librarian-retrieval-service; edge creation is T-librarian-edge-maintenance
**Test plan:** write a memory via endpoint; verify tags extracted and stored; verify EmbeddingPayload present with vector + model metadata in payloads jsonb; verify source_agent recorded; verify second write from different source_agent stores differently
